title: Quiz: Pauli Gates
description: Test your understanding of the Pauli X, Y, and Z gates

questions.question: What is the result of applying the Pauli X gate to |0⟩?
questions.explanation: The X gate is the quantum NOT gate. It flips |0⟩ to |1⟩ and vice versa. X|0⟩ = |1⟩.

questions.question: What is the result of Z|1⟩?
questions.explanation: The Z gate applies a phase flip to |1⟩. Z|0⟩ = |0⟩ and Z|1⟩ = -|1⟩. It introduces a relative phase of π.

questions.question: Which equation correctly describes the action of the Y gate on |0⟩?
questions.explanation: The Y gate combines a bit flip and phase operations. Y|0⟩ = i|1⟩ and Y|1⟩ = -i|0⟩.

questions.question: For all Pauli gates P, P² = I (identity).
questions.explanation: True! All Pauli matrices are involutory, meaning X² = Y² = Z² = I. This means each Pauli gate is its own inverse.

questions.question: What is the commutator [X, Y] = XY - YX?
questions.explanation: The Pauli matrices satisfy [X,Y] = 2iZ, [Y,Z] = 2iX, and [Z,X] = 2iY. These cyclic commutation relations are fundamental.

questions.question: What is XY (the product of X and Y)?
questions.explanation: XY = iZ. The Pauli matrices satisfy XY = iZ, YZ = iX, and ZX = iY (cyclic with factor i).

questions.question: On the Bloch sphere, the Pauli X gate corresponds to:
questions.explanation: The Pauli X gate is a 180° (π) rotation around the X-axis. Similarly, Y is 180° around Y-axis, and Z is 180° around Z-axis.

questions.question: Which of the following properties are true for all Pauli matrices (X, Y, Z)? Select all that apply.
questions.explanation: All Pauli matrices are Hermitian, unitary, and traceless. However, their determinant is -1, not 1 (det(X) = det(Y) = det(Z) = -1).
